en.extract:
The moustached babbler (Malacopteron magnirostre) is a species of bird in the ground babbler family Pellorneidae. The species is also known as the brown-headed babbler or brown-headed tree-babbler.


Taxonomy and systematics
This species has two subspecies, the nominate race M. m. magnirostre and the subspecies M. m. cinereocapilla (Salvadori, 1868). A third subspecies, flavum,  from the Anamba Islands, is sometimes recognised but is usually merged into the nominate race. The specific name magnirostre comes from the Latin magnus for big and rostris for billed. The name for the subspecies cinereocapilla comes from the Latin cinereus for ash/ashy and capillus for headed.


Distribution and habitat
The moustached babbler is found in Sundaland. The nominate race is found in southern Myanmar and Thailand through Peninsular Malaysia and Sumatra, and the subspecies M. m. cinereocapilla is endemic to Borneo. Formerly, it could be found in the forests of Singapore, but it is now likely extinct there.
Its natural habitat is tropical moist primary lowland forests, peatswamp forest, secondary forest, logged forests and old rubber plantations, from 915 m (3,002 ft), rarely to 1,200 m (3,900 ft). It is suggested that this species, since it prefers logged forest to primary forest, could be used as a habitat indicator.


Description
The moustached babbler is 18 cm (7.1 in) long and weighs between 16–25 g (0.56–0.88 oz). The plumage is dull brown above and whitish below. The crown is olive-brown in the nominate race, with a grey loral stripe and moustachial stripe.


Behaviour
The moustached babbler feeds on insects, including on beetles and locusts. They feed in mid-story, from 4–6 m (13–20 ft) off the ground.


References


External links
BirdLife fact sheet

The '''moustached babbler''' ('''''Malacopteron magnirostre''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the ground babbler family [[Pellorneidae]]. The species is also known as the '''brown-headed babbler''' or '''brown-headed tree-babbler'''.

==Taxonomy and systematics==
This species has two subspecies, the nominate race ''M. m. magnirostre'' and the subspecies ''M. m. cinereocapilla'' ([[Tommaso Salvadori|Salvadori]], 1868). A third subspecies, ''flavum'',  from the [[Anamba Islands]], is sometimes recognised but is usually merged into the nominate race. The specific name ''magnirostre'' comes from the [[Latin]] ''magnus'' for big and ''rostris'' for billed. The name for the subspecies ''cinereocapilla'' comes from the Latin ''cinereus'' for ash/ashy and ''capillus'' for headed.<ref name="HBW" >Collar, N. & Robson, C. (2017). Moustached Babbler (''Malacopteron magnirostre''). In: del Hoyo, J., Elliott, A., Sargatal, J., Christie, D.A. & de Juana, E. (eds.). Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona. (retrieved from http://www.hbw.com/node/59505 on 18 March 2017).</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==
The moustached babbler is found in [[Sundaland]]. The nominate race is found in southern Myanmar and Thailand through Peninsular Malaysia and [[Sumatra]], and the subspecies ''M. m. cinereocapilla'' is [[endemism|endemic]] to [[Borneo]].<ref name="HBW" /> Formerly, it could be found in the forests of [[Singapore]], but it is now likely extinct there.<ref name="Yong2009">{{cite journal|first=D. L.|last=Yong|title=Persistence of babbler (Timaliidae) communities in Singapore forests|journal=Nature in Singapore|year=2009|volume=2|pages=365–371|url=http://rmbr.nus.edu.sg/nis/bulletin2009/2009nis365-371.pdf|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120616094848/http://rmbr.nus.edu.sg/nis/bulletin2009/2009nis365-371.pdf|archive-date=2012-06-16}}</ref>

Its natural [[habitat]] is tropical moist primary lowland [[forest]]s, peatswamp forest, secondary forest, logged forests and old rubber plantations, from {{convert|915|m|ft|abbr=on}}, rarely to {{convert|1200|m|ft|abbr=on}}.<ref name="HBW" /> It is suggested that this species, since it prefers logged forest to primary forest, could be used as a habitat indicator.{{Citation needed|date=December 2019|reason=removed citation to predatory publisher content}}

==Description==
The moustached babbler is {{convert|18|cm|in|abbr=on}} long and weighs between {{convert|16|-|25|g|abbr=on}}. The plumage is dull brown above and whitish below. The crown is olive-brown in the nominate race, with a grey loral stripe and {{birdgloss|moustachial stripe}}.<ref name="HBW" />

==Behaviour==
The moustached babbler feeds on insects, including on beetles and locusts. They feed in mid-story, from {{convert|4|–|6|m|ft|abbr=on}} off the ground.<ref name="HBW" />

zh.extract:
须树鹛（学名：Malacopteron magnirostre）是画眉科树鹛属的一种，分布于文莱、缅甸、泰国、印度尼西亚、马来西亚和新加坡。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
须树鹛的平均体重约为19.4克。栖息地包括亚热带或热带严重退化的前森林、亚热带或热带的湿润低地林、亚热带或热带的高海拔疏灌丛和亚热带或热带的沼泽林。


参考文献
